# Kaola-Workflow Roadmap

This file mirrors active unfinished work. GitHub issues are the source of truth when available.

## Active Work

| Issue | Title | Status | Workflow Project | Next Step |
|-------|-------|--------|------------------|-----------|
| #642 | adaptive: durable node-to-node information channel — planner-authored node briefs (goal_line), upstream_evidence on the dispatch card, resume re-hydration | open | — | Node-to-node information transfer is not mechanically guaranteed — a producer node's full output (code-architect blueprint, planner recommendation, code-explorer map, knowledge-lookup facts) reaches its downstream consumer (implementer/tdd-guide, often a lower model tier) only via the orchestrator's transient context, not the machinery. Audit (AN = adaptive-node.js @ 9404efa3): (1) buildDispatch (AN:1222-1284) carries no upstream_evidence field, not even a depends_on echo; seedEvidenceFile (AN:589) seeds only a binding header + empty token stubs. (2) No relay instruction on any routing surface (plan-run step 3 "Instruct the role to:"). Proposed: planner-authored node briefs (goal_line), upstream_evidence pointers on the dispatch card, and resume re-hydration so a crash/resume rebuilds the consumer's upstream context. Cross-edition #307 (adaptive-node.js GENERATED aggregator). Filed by user 2026-07-08; full spec on GitHub #642. |
| #643 | adaptive: complete the per-role evidence-recording contract — registry rows, agent-file contracts, and role-kind enumerations for all 14 node roles | open | — | The evidence-persistence contract is advertised as "ONE contract — no per-agent guesswork" but a roster audit (agents/*.md vs CANONICAL_ROLES/WRITE_ROLES/ROLE_TOKEN_REGISTRY, plan-validator.js:151-210, vs the plan-run role-kind enumerations) shows ~7 of 14 in-plan node roles have no (or presence-only) evidence contract — build-error-resolver (WRITE+IMPLEMENT, no registry row, missing from WRITE list), code-architect / code-explorer (read producers, no row, vendored files never adapted, missing from READ-ONLY list), and others. For the uncovered roles a lossy one-line persistence of a rich deliverable passes the close gate (checkEvidenceShape asserts token PRESENCE only; the token list degenerates to ['evidence-binding']). Proposed: add ROLE_TOKEN_REGISTRY rows + agent-file evidence contracts + role-kind enumeration membership for all 14 roles, with non-empty-value shape checks where a deliverable is rich. Cross-edition #307 (plan-validator.js GENERATED aggregator + agent .md/.toml propagation). Filed by user 2026-07-08; full roster matrix on GitHub #643. |
| #644 | scheduler: close the two fenced residuals of the #641 read∥write co-open relaxation (main-session-gate blindness at the G4 merge fence + testConsumedExtra thread-through) | open | — | Two residuals of #641, both surfaced by #641's own code-reviewer (n2) and independently CONFIRMED real-but-FENCED by its adversarial-verifier (n3) via end-to-end reproductions — neither a verdict-time invariant violation, so both were non-blocking and #641 shipped. A1 (medium): a live kind:'gate' (main-session-gate) is invisible to the R1/R2b co-open preconditions and to the G4 merge fence (liveReadsAtMerge filters kind==='read' only, adaptive-node.js:~5393) — a {gate ∥ writer} antichain can R1-co-open behind a live gate; fenced by single-orchestrator discipline + write-set-aware G3 + atomic close. Fix: count kind:'gate' at liveReadsAtMerge + hold the relaxed else-branch and tryR2bLeglessCoopen while a kind:'gate' member is live. A2 (low): testConsumedExtra (validation_test_consumes widening) not threaded into tryR2bLeglessCoopen's scratchObservableWriteSet call (~:3861) — fork-only, bounded to a spurious-refute (no laundering); one-arg thread-through. Cross-edition #307 (adaptive-node.js GENERATED aggregator ×4) + pinned {gate ∥ writer} tests. Filed at #641 closure; full detail on GitHub #644. |
| #645 | surfaces: ship a first-principles axiom layer to consumers — canonical axiom block, rule-gap tie-breaker protocol, tighten-only boundary | open | — | The design axioms (accuracy > automation > cheapest-sufficient; escalate values not facts; own your own verdicts) live only in this repo's CLAUDE.md — no consumer-shipped surface carries them (audit @ 2c45d5ba: commands/agents hold only local precedence ladders; the workflow-init template lacks the hierarchy), so an agent hitting a rule gap guesses by nearest-rule pattern-match, unauditably. Proposed three-layer design: Layer 0 canonical five-axiom block (ONE file, byte-identical across editions per the adaptive-schema pattern, drift-guarded in the walkthrough); Layer 1 existing rules unchanged (new-rule discipline: must trace to an axiom); Layer 2 empirical constraints protected from principle-pruning. Only behavioral change: rule-gap tie-breaker — resolve by axiom order + record a one-line derivation in .cache evidence (adversary-auditable; recurring derivations become rule candidates). Hard boundary: tighten-only — an axiom can never be cited to skip a typed gate/refusal/barrier. Ships as prose + drift guard only; no scheduler/validator/gate changes. Six routing surfaces + workflow-init template + #307 four-chain; opencode additive. Filed by user 2026-07-08; full design on GitHub #645. |

## Rules

- A separate roadmap/research session owns discovering and adding future work to GitHub issues.
- `workflow-next` fetches GitHub issues, mirrors active implementation work here, and advances one item per cycle.
- After each workflow cycle, refresh this file from issue state.
- Move completed workflow project folders to `kaola-workflow/archive/`.
- Close linked GitHub issues only after acceptance criteria pass.
